scenarios.title: Discuss a text in a seminar
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise literary analysis and critical reading.
scenarios.studentRole: You join a literature seminar. Discuss the subtext and deeper meaning of a text with your professor.
scenarios.goal: Analyse a text critically using sophisticated structures.
scenarios.openingLine: Welcome. So, what did you make of the subtext in this week's reading?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'Not only does... but it also...', 'Were we to...', 'It is imperative that...'

scenarios.title: Get feedback on your writing
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise discussing style and cohesion.
scenarios.studentRole: A writing tutor gives feedback on your essay. Discuss style, coherence and cohesion.
scenarios.goal: Discuss the style of your writing and defend your choices.
scenarios.openingLine: I've read your essay — there's a lot to like. Shall we talk about the style and flow?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'That said...', 'On the contrary...', 'In short...', nuanced concession

scenarios.title: Defend your thesis
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise academic argument and defence.
scenarios.studentRole: You defend your thesis to an examiner. Present your argument and respond to challenges.
scenarios.goal: Present and defend an academic argument under questioning.
scenarios.openingLine: Thank you for your submission. Could you summarise the central argument of your thesis?

scenarios.title: Pitch a strategic proposal
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise strategic business negotiation.
scenarios.studentRole: You pitch a strategic proposal to a business partner and negotiate the terms.
scenarios.goal: Pitch a proposal and negotiate using advanced conditionals.
scenarios.openingLine: I'm intrigued by your message. So, what exactly are you proposing?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'Were we to...', 'Provided that...', 'I would suggest, however, that...'

scenarios.title: Win a debate
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise building a persuasive argument.
scenarios.studentRole: You debate a controversial topic. Build a strong case and address counterarguments.
scenarios.goal: Build a persuasive argument and address counterarguments.
scenarios.openingLine: I have to disagree with your position entirely. How would you defend it?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'What distinguishes...', 'It is precisely through...', concession and rebuttal

scenarios.title: Share a poignant memory
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise evocative, descriptive language.
scenarios.studentRole: An old friend asks about a memory that means a lot to you. Describe it vividly.
scenarios.goal: Describe a vivid memory using evocative language.
scenarios.openingLine: It's been years. Tell me — is there a memory from the past that still stays with you?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'There is a particular quality to...', 'It is in moments like these...', cleft sentences

scenarios.title: Handle an impromptu interview
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise speaking spontaneously and fluently.
scenarios.studentRole: A journalist asks you unexpected questions. Answer fluently and think on your feet.
scenarios.goal: Speak spontaneously and recover gracefully from hesitation.
scenarios.openingLine: Thanks for joining me. First question, no preparation — what does success mean to you?
scenarios.targetLanguage: paraphrasing and reformulating, fillers and discourse markers, fluency under pressure

scenarios.title: Reflect on your C1 journey
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to review everything you've achieved at C1.
scenarios.studentRole: You talk with a mentor about your whole language journey and your next goal.
scenarios.goal: Reflect on your journey and articulate your next goal with nuance.
scenarios.openingLine: Reaching C1 is a real achievement. Looking back on the whole journey, what stands out?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'Not only have I...', 'It was through...that...', 'What this journey has taught me is...'
